response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web engineer with over 15 years of experience, but he is mismatched with nearly every core requirement of this embedded networking security role. Most critically, no Japanese language proficiency is listed anywhere — a stated hard requirement — and his primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is entirely web-focused with no evidence of C-centric embedded firmware work, networking security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal), Linux kernel internals, or LAN-switch domain knowledge.
strengths: Extensive career spanning 15+ years across multiple domains, including some embedded/hardware exposure at Altom Consulting and National Instruments
Peripheral C/C++ experience from NI LabWindows and a bachelor thesis project involving Linux, Jack audio, and multi-speaker hardware
Demonstrated comfort with Linux environments and REST APIs, which are listed as preferred skills
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed in any form — this is an explicit hard requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero experience with embedded networking security technologies required by the role (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, tamper detection)
C programming appears only as peripheral exposure at two early roles; the candidate's sustained expertise is in web/full-stack technologies irrelevant to this position

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
